target: 8. Tokiko's Memory Part 2
Tokiko Hishigata
(That phone call just now... Haine is serious...)
(Become a Shadow, huh...)
(If we're going to stop Shinpei-san and the others, my surprise attack, since I'm not suspected yet, would definitely be effective.)
(But... being told something so important so suddenly, I can't just...)
Tokiko.
...Mom.
What's wrong? Your face looks pale.
It's nothing.
No, it's not nothing. Let's talk with Mom for a bit.
Yeah…
Were you thinking about Ushio-chan?
You still can't believe it, huh... Even though she was so good friends with both Sou and you.
…Yeah.
You must have been really lonely. You wanted to go to the festival together, didn't you?
Y-Yeah... Ugh... uuuh...!
N-No... It's not like... I'm crying... uugh...!

Hehe, it's okay to cry.
You don't have to hold it in.
But... p-please don't tell... Dad and older brother...
Yes, yes, it's alright.
Ugh, uuuu...! You know, Mom...
I had fun! I really had fun...!
I wanted... I wanted it to stay like that forever!

Why!?
Why did this have to happen…!
I know...
I loved them, you know...? Ushio-san... Shinpei-san too... Ugh, uuugh.
Yeah, yeah.
I love everyone... Older brother, Dad, Mom... Even Mio...!
You're such a kind girl, Tokiko.
Uku... No, I'm not kind at all...

(That's why I was chosen.
Not my older brother, but me…)

Hey, Tokiko.
You've got something else on your mind, don't you?

Eh…?
No, nothing…
You can't fool your mother's eyes, you know? Even before what happened to Ushio-chan, you sometimes had a troubled look on your face.
Or is it something you can't tell Mom?
I'm sorry... It's not just Mom, I can't tell anyone...
Not even Mio-chan?
Yeah... I absolutely can't tell her.
…Hehe.
Why are you laughing…?
I was just thinking you're stubborn, just like your dad. But... let's see.
Can't you distance yourself from the source of that worry you can't tell anyone about?
I can't do that...
(Nursing Haine, giving her food... I felt like we were making an equal trade.)
(But the truth is different... Their position is definitely higher... I absolutely can't ignore what they ask of me.)
(Besides, at this point... If I defy them, even Mio will...!)
There's nothing I can do anymore.
Is that so... If I could, I'd take your place for you.
But I'm sure it'll be okay!
Huh...?
Because you're a girl who can think about a lot of different things, Tokiko.
That's why you should think carefully, and decide with your own free will.
…Yeah.
Make sure you don't regret it, okay?
Hehe... it's strange.
Looking at your smiling face, Mom, makes me feel like it'll be okay.
Thank you, Mom... I'm so happy you're here for me.
Oh, really? Well, that makes me happy too.
Thank you. You really are my Mom.
Don't say silly things. There's no way anyone other than me could be your Mom.
Hehe… that's right.
(Even if it's out of self-interest, I don't care. I will protect both Mom and Mio.)
(And once we get through the day of the festival...)
(We can be together as a family forever...)
It seems I was overthinking things a bit. But I'm okay now. I think it'll work out.
Hehe, since you've cheered up, Tokiko, I'll tell you something good.
Huh...?
Actually, I have your favorite... candied violets.
Really!?
It's a bit late, but shall we have some tea together? Of course, keep it a secret from your dad and older brother.
Thank you. I'm really happy, but I can't right now. There's something I have to do.
Oh, is that so? Then let's do it tomorrow.
Yeah… see you tomorrow.
You're there, aren't you?
I don't know whose Shadow you are, but can we get this over with quickly?
It's quite something. No matter how you look at it, you look just like me.
Of course. If you weren't here, I'd be the real one.
That's true. Well then, make sure I don't suffer.
Yeah, I know. Make sure you don't leave any regrets behind.
Hehe. I know—
Goodbye, me.
